editor_note: This week's digest centres on two contrasting developments: new tools that help people build practical AI skills for the workplace, and a significant policy shift in which the United States has restricted foreign access to some of the most advanced AI models. Together they highlight both the growing opportunities for workforce development and the evolving regulations that shape who can use these technologies. As always, we focus on what these changes mean for our education and human-capital mandate.

items.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
items.summary: OpenAI has launched three free online courses through its Academy to help people learn practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI 'agents' for everyday tasks. The courses are aimed at general workers rather than technical specialists.
items.why_it_matters: These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's upskilling and workforce-development programmes with accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.

items.title: US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera
items.summary: Al Jazeera reports that the US government has asked AI company Anthropic to block global access to its most advanced models, and explains the reasoning behind the move. The decision reflects growing national-security concerns over who can use powerful AI tools.
items.why_it_matters: Restrictions on advanced AI access can directly affect which tools are available to organisations in the UAE and wider region, including for education and public-sector use.

items.title: U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times
items.summary: The New York Times reports that the United States is barring foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most capable AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to frontier AI technology on security grounds.
items.why_it_matters: As a UAE government institution, Tamkeen should be aware that access to leading AI models may now depend on nationality and location, affecting planning and tool choices.

items.title: US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera
items.summary: According to Al Jazeera, the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure widens earlier restrictions and signals a stricter approach to controlling advanced AI access internationally.
items.why_it_matters: Such broad restrictions underline the importance of building regional and local AI capacity so the UAE is not dependent on tools that may suddenly become unavailable.

items.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
items.summary: Language-learning platform Preply is using OpenAI's technology to offer AI-generated lesson summaries, personalised feedback, and tailored exercises alongside its human tutors. The aim is to combine automated support with real teachers for more personalised learning.
items.why_it_matters: This is a practical example of blending AI with human instruction, relevant to Tamkeen's interest in personalised education and language and skills training.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.summary: OpenAI says it is supporting the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI transparency, which focuses on helping people identify AI-generated content. The effort includes developing standards and tools to show where digital content comes from.
items.why_it_matters: Content-transparency standards are increasingly relevant to public-sector communication and education, helping institutions and the public trust the information they receive.

items.title: Anthropic Pulls Its Most Powerful AI Models After U.S. Bars Foreign Access - Time Magazine
items.summary: Time Magazine reports that Anthropic has withdrawn its most powerful AI models after the US restricted foreign access to them. The company has effectively pulled these tools rather than maintaining limited availability.
items.why_it_matters: The sudden removal of leading models shows how quickly access can change, reinforcing the value of diversified and regionally available AI resources.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC
items.summary: The BBC reports that two of Anthropic's advanced AI systems, named Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, have been suspended over security concerns. The suspension is linked to the wider restrictions on access to these powerful models.
items.why_it_matters: Knowing which specific tools are affected helps Tamkeen plan around possible disruptions when selecting AI services for training and operations.

items.text: ● DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review
AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Week of 11–17 June 2026

body.text: This week's digest centres on two contrasting developments: new tools that help people build practical AI skills for the workplace, and a significant policy shift in which the United States has restricted foreign access to some of the most advanced AI models. Together they highlight both the growing opportunities for workforce development and the evolving regulations that shape who can use these technologies. As always, we focus on what these changes mean for our education and human-capital mandate.

items.text: [1. New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work](https://openai.com/index/academy-courses-applying-ai-at-work)

OpenAI has launched three free online courses through its Academy to help people learn practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI 'agents' for everyday tasks. The courses are aimed at general workers rather than technical specialists.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's upskilling and workforce-development programmes with accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.
OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026

[2. US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera](https://news.google.?oc=5)

Al Jazeera reports that the US government has asked AI company Anthropic to block global access to its most advanced models, and explains the reasoning behind the move. The decision reflects growing national-security concerns over who can use powerful AI tools.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Restrictions on advanced AI access can directly affect which tools are available to organisations in the UAE and wider region, including for education and public-sector use.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 14 Jun 2026

[3. U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxPTHRjTk4ySzZxRVVlTnA4LXdBSzZ3RzFEX0puLW1ZTVhxNXRSTmN3ZEVhV0phNWF5YzhfMVhZWGhnR0RlMzhDeUx6RkhuWXRYR0YyTWVTdU1EX3ZZV3l5S3MtRW5LbHczR2VlWDRVSjlVcnRKVTY4Y0ZmZ1V5bWdpb1dVQXBTLTdTMUE?oc=5)

The New York Times reports that the United States is barring foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most capable AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to frontier AI technology on security grounds.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** As a UAE government institution, Tamkeen should be aware that access to leading AI models may now depend on nationality and location, affecting planning and tool choices.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026

[4. US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera](https://news.google.?oc=5)

According to Al Jazeera, the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure widens earlier restrictions and signals a stricter approach to controlling advanced AI access internationally.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Such broad restrictions underline the importance of building regional and local AI capacity so the UAE is not dependent on tools that may suddenly become unavailable.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026
[5. How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning](https://openai.com/index/preply)

Language-learning platform Preply is using OpenAI's technology to offer AI-generated lesson summaries, personalised feedback, and tailored exercises alongside its human tutors. The aim is to combine automated support with real teachers for more personalised learning.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** This is a practical example of blending AI with human instruction, relevant to Tamkeen's interest in personalised education and language and skills training.
OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026
[6. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem](https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem)

OpenAI says it is supporting the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI transparency, which focuses on helping people identify AI-generated content. The effort includes developing standards and tools to show where digital content comes from.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Content-transparency standards are increasingly relevant to public-sector communication and education, helping institutions and the public trust the information they receive.
OpenAI · 11 Jun 2026

[7. Anthropic Pulls Its Most Powerful AI Models After U.S. Bars Foreign Access - Time Magazine](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMigAFBVV95cUxQb3dwQVpWdEY5V28zYjAzTXY2Xzl0MDV2RDRrNTBvNVhYMGlzVzJac3k3SlRTMWh1SnpsNDJ4M0pTRXRZclkxNng0WTdCMTRLOUJ2ZkNxTE5SNG11SkZWQ3EzNWZvYnFOYVFrdmRhWGlJcGpIY0htUjVXQXFlSHM1bg?oc=5)

Time Magazine reports that Anthropic has withdrawn its most powerful AI models after the US restricted foreign access to them. The company has effectively pulled these tools rather than maintaining limited availability.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** The sudden removal of leading models shows how quickly access can change, reinforcing the value of diversified and regionally available AI resources.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026

[8. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiWkFVX3lxTE1iZTNFalowWHVUV2FhOGlEcWgxVWdISzBVLXJfN2FXOXBjdkVhRG0wVFRyNURuMHNxREtKeENYSkdWdm45OXBTd2xaUzlZQjBTQ2dQWjgzOUtCZw?oc=5)

The BBC reports that two of Anthropic's advanced AI systems, named Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, have been suspended over security concerns. The suspension is linked to the wider restrictions on access to these powerful models.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Knowing which specific tools are affected helps Tamkeen plan around possible disruptions when selecting AI services for training and operations.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

plain_text:
*** DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review ***

AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Week of 11–17 June 2026
============================================================
This week's digest centres on two contrasting developments: new tools that help people build practical AI skills for the workplace, and a significant policy shift in which the United States has restricted foreign access to some of the most advanced AI models. Together they highlight both the growing opportunities for workforce development and the evolving regulations that shape who can use these technologies. As always, we focus on what these changes mean for our education and human-capital mandate.

1. New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
   https://openai.com/index/academy-courses-applying-ai-at-work
   OpenAI has launched three free online courses through its Academy to help people learn practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI 'agents' for everyday tasks. The courses are aimed at general workers rather than technical specialists.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's upskilling and workforce-development programmes with accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.
   (OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026)

2. US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera
   https://news.google.?oc=5
   Al Jazeera reports that the US government has asked AI company Anthropic to block global access to its most advanced models, and explains the reasoning behind the move. The decision reflects growing national-security concerns over who can use powerful AI tools.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Restrictions on advanced AI access can directly affect which tools are available to organisations in the UAE and wider region, including for education and public-sector use.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 14 Jun 2026)

3. U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxPTHRjTk4ySzZxRVVlTnA4LXdBSzZ3RzFEX0puLW1ZTVhxNXRSTmN3ZEVhV0phNWF5YzhfMVhZWGhnR0RlMzhDeUx6RkhuWXRYR0YyTWVTdU1EX3ZZV3l5S3MtRW5LbHczR2VlWDRVSjlVcnRKVTY4Y0ZmZ1V5bWdpb1dVQXBTLTdTMUE?oc=5
   The New York Times reports that the United States is barring foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most capable AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to frontier AI technology on security grounds.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: As a UAE government institution, Tamkeen should be aware that access to leading AI models may now depend on nationality and location, affecting planning and tool choices.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

4. US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera
   https://news.google.?oc=5
   According to Al Jazeera, the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure widens earlier restrictions and signals a stricter approach to controlling advanced AI access internationally.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Such broad restrictions underline the importance of building regional and local AI capacity so the UAE is not dependent on tools that may suddenly become unavailable.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

5. How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
   https://openai.com/index/preply
   Language-learning platform Preply is using OpenAI's technology to offer AI-generated lesson summaries, personalised feedback, and tailored exercises alongside its human tutors. The aim is to combine automated support with real teachers for more personalised learning.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: This is a practical example of blending AI with human instruction, relevant to Tamkeen's interest in personalised education and language and skills training.
   (OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026)

6. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
   https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem
   OpenAI says it is supporting the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI transparency, which focuses on helping people identify AI-generated content. The effort includes developing standards and tools to show where digital content comes from.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Content-transparency standards are increasingly relevant to public-sector communication and education, helping institutions and the public trust the information they receive.
   (OpenAI · 11 Jun 2026)

7. Anthropic Pulls Its Most Powerful AI Models After U.S. Bars Foreign Access - Time Magazine
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMigAFBVV95cUxQb3dwQVpWdEY5V28zYjAzTXY2Xzl0MDV2RDRrNTBvNVhYMGlzVzJac3k3SlRTMWh1SnpsNDJ4M0pTRXRZclkxNng0WTdCMTRLOUJ2ZkNxTE5SNG11SkZWQ3EzNWZvYnFOYVFrdmRhWGlJcGpIY0htUjVXQXFlSHM1bg?oc=5
   Time Magazine reports that Anthropic has withdrawn its most powerful AI models after the US restricted foreign access to them. The company has effectively pulled these tools rather than maintaining limited availability.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: The sudden removal of leading models shows how quickly access can change, reinforcing the value of diversified and regionally available AI resources.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

8. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiWkFVX3lxTE1iZTNFalowWHVUV2FhOGlEcWgxVWdISzBVLXJfN2FXOXBjdkVhRG0wVFRyNURuMHNxREtKeENYSkdWdm45OXBTd2xaUzlZQjBTQ2dQWjgzOUtCZw?oc=5
   The BBC reports that two of Anthropic's advanced AI systems, named Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, have been suspended over security concerns. The suspension is linked to the wider restrictions on access to these powerful models.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Knowing which specific tools are affected helps Tamkeen plan around possible disruptions when selecting AI services for training and operations.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

------------------------------------------------------------
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

[6. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem](https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem)

[7. Time Magazine reports that Anthropic has withdrawn its most powerful AI models after the US restricted foreign access to them. The company has effectively pulled these tools rather than maintaining limited availability.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** The sudden removal of leading models shows how quickly access can change, reinforcing the value of diversified and regionally available AI resources.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026

[8. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI suspended over security fears - BBC](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiWkFVX3lxTE1iZTNFalowWHVUV2FhOGlEcWgxVWdISzBVLXJfN2FXOXBjdkVhRG0wVFRyNURuMHNxREtKeENYSkdWdm45OXBTd2xaUzlZQjBTQ2dQWjgzOUtCZw?oc=5)
